sections.paragraph:
const. st. אֶנְקַת f.

(1) clamour, groaning (Angftgefchreh), Mal 2:13 as of captives, Psa 79:11 102:21 of the wretched, 12:6 .

(2) Lev 11:30 a kind of reptiles of the lizard race, taking their name from the groaning noise like an exclamation of grief, which some lizards make. LXX., Vulg. render it mus araneus, shrew mouse. See Bochart (Hieroz. i. 1068, seq.)
